behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: request-builder + API client wiring. A pure Unit test of only a formatter can miss dropped/renamed fields at the call boundary. Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess is required (stub client can capture outbound payload), no dependence on model output quality, and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module with a stubbed API client that records outbound payload. Invoke classify() with a taxonomy and assert the transmitted request contains every taxonomy label (no omissions, no substitutions) before client send.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Real failure can occur at parser/normalizer/module boundary when raw provider output is handed off and validated. Unit-testing only a helper risks missing mapping not being applied in the real call path. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about handling an out-of-taxonomy value, not model quality; stub response is sufficient, so no System/Agentic requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Run classifier with stub API returning an invalid label (e.g., "healthcare") and taxonomy [legal, technical, financial, other]. Assert public classify() result is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly isolates normalization determinism for identical raw input. This is a single local logic boundary with no I/O and no module interaction required.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function/object repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and taxonomy; assert identical normalized label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live AI-provider calls and consistency of returned labels. Mocking hides real model/provider variability and cannot catch this failure class. Rejected Workflow: this is repeated single-step classification calls, not a multi-step agent pipeline with dependent model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Against real provider, issue N repeated classify() calls for identical document+taxonomy, recording raw provider responses and final labels. Assert: if raw provider response is unchanged across adjacent calls, final label is unchanged; only response changes may explain label changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure is at interaction boundary between token-budget enforcement and dispatch path (send vs truncate/reject). Testing only the budget helper can miss accidental dispatch despite over-budget state. Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no dependence on model output quality; no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Configure low token budget and stub API client. Exercise over-budget document for each policy (truncate/reject). Assert request is truncated or blocked per policy before send, and dispatch behavior matches policy.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Needs real external API token accounting and real HTTP response metadata; mocks can trivially pass while hiding provider-side tokenization/usage differences. Rejected Agentic: behavior text targets provider-reported usage/budget compliance, not semantic quality of model output or model decision correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run classify() against live provider with representative document and configured budget. Assert provider-reported usage tokens in real response metadata are <= configured budget for that call.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is retry orchestration (rate-limit signal -> wait/backoff -> retry -> success) across local modules; reproducible with deterministic stubbed API and fake clock. Rejected System: behavior does not require real network conditions, only correct handling of a rate-limit response contract. No model-output dependence, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Use stub API returning rate-limit error on first call and success on second, with controllable clock/sleeper. Assert classifier waits/backs off, retries once or per policy, and returns successful label instead of surfacing immediate error.
